0.title: Birth of Muhammad
0.desc: Born in Mecca into the Hashim clan of the Quraysh tribe.

0.title: First Revelation
0.desc: Receives the first verses of the Quran at Mount Hira near Mecca.

0.title: The Hijrah
0.desc: Migration from Mecca to Medina; marks the beginning of the Islamic (Hijri) calendar.

0.title: Battle of Badr
0.desc: The first major military victory for the early Muslim community.

0.title: Treaty of Hudaybiyyah
0.desc: A pivotal 10-year peace treaty between Muslims and the Quraysh.

0.title: Conquest of Mecca
0.desc: The city is peacefully integrated; the Kaaba is rededicated to monotheism.

0.title: Farewell Pilgrimage
0.desc: The Prophet's final pilgrimage and sermon; he passes away shortly after.

1.title: Election of Abu Bakr
1.desc: Becomes the first Caliph; suppresses the Ridda (Apostasy) wars to unify Arabia.

1.title: Caliphate of Umar
1.desc: Rapid expansion into Persia, Egypt, and the Levant; Battle of Yarmouk (636).

1.title: Hijri Calendar established
1.desc: Caliph Umar formalizes the Islamic calendar starting from the Hijrah.

1.title: Uthmanic Codex
1.desc: The third Caliph Uthman standardizes the written text of the Quran.

1.title: Battle of the Camel
1.desc: First civil war (Fitna) in Islamic history; Ali ibn Abi Talib moves the capital to Kufa.

1.title: Assassination of Ali
1.desc: Marks the end of the Rashidun era and the rise of the Umayyad dynasty.

2.title: Umayyad Dynasty begins
2.desc: Capital moves to Damascus; Muawiyah I establishes hereditary rule.

2.title: Dome of the Rock
2.desc: Completed in Jerusalem under Caliph Abd al-Malik.

2.title: Spain & India Expansion
2.desc: Tariq ibn Ziyad enters Iberia; Muhammad ibn Qasim reaches the Indus.

2.title: Abbasid Revolution
2.desc: The Abbasids overthrow the Umayyads, shifting focus to Baghdad.

2.title: Founding of Baghdad
2.desc: The 'Round City' becomes the world's premier center of learning.

2.title: House of Wisdom
2.desc: Al-Ma'mun expands the Bayt al-Hikma for scientific and philosophical translation.

2.title: Siege of Baghdad
2.desc: Mongols under Hulagu Khan sack the city, ending the Abbasid Golden Age.

3.title: Fatimid Caliphate
3.desc: A Shia caliphate centered in Cairo; built the Al-Azhar University.

3.title: Battle of Manzikert
3.desc: Seljuk Turks defeat the Byzantines, opening Anatolia to settlement.

3.title: Start of the Crusades
3.desc: Pope Urban II calls for the First Crusade to capture Jerusalem.

3.title: Saladin's Victory
3.desc: Ayyubid Sultan Saladin recaptures Jerusalem after the Battle of Hattin.

3.title: Battle of Ain Jalut
3.desc: The Mamluks of Egypt decisively stop the Mongol westward advance.

3.title: Ibn Battuta's Travels
3.desc: The world-renowned explorer completes his journeys across Africa, Asia, and Europe.

4.title: Fall of Constantinople
4.desc: Mehmed the Conqueror takes the city, making it the Ottoman capital, Istanbul.

4.title: Safavid State founded
4.desc: Shah Ismail I establishes the Safavid Empire with Twelver Shi'ism as the state religion.

4.title: Mughal Empire founded
4.desc: Babur defeats the Delhi Sultanate at the First Battle of Panipat.

4.title: Suleiman the Magnificent
4.desc: The Ottoman Empire reaches its peak in law, administration, and territory.

4.title: Completion of Taj Mahal
4.desc: Built by Mughal Emperor Shah Jahan as a tomb for his wife Mumtaz Mahal.

4.title: Second Siege of Vienna
4.desc: A major turning point marking the beginning of Ottoman territorial stagnation in Europe.

4.title: Abolition of the Caliphate
4.desc: The Turkish Grand National Assembly formally ends the Ottoman Caliphate.

5.title: Independence of Pakistan
5.desc: The creation of a sovereign state for Muslims in South Asia.

5.title: Formation of the OIC
5.desc: The Organization of Islamic Cooperation is established to represent the collective voice of Muslims.

5.title: Islamic Solidarity Fund
5.desc: Established to support educational and cultural projects worldwide.

5.title: Iranian Revolution
5.desc: The establishment of the Islamic Republic of Iran under Ayatollah Khomeini.

5.title: Technological Growth
5.desc: Rapid modernization in the Gulf states and technological shifts across the Muslim world.

5.title: Global Population
5.desc: Muslims reach over 1.9 billion worldwide, living in over 50 majority nations.
